channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Los Angeles prosecutors to review new evidence in Menendez brothers' 1996 murder conviction
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Prosecutors in Los Angeles are reviewing new evidence in the case of Erik and Lyle Menendez to determine whether they should be serving life sentences for killing their parents in their Beverly Hills mansion more than 35 years ago, the city's district attorney said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles DA George Gascon will review new evidence in Menendez brothers murder case
item.description: New evidence was presented to the Los Angeles County District Attorney's Office that needs to be reviewed in the Menendez brothers case, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man faces charges after he called 911 on someone who lit a scented candle: police
item.description: A New Jersey man is facing charges after fabricating both mental health and fire concerns over someone lighting a scented candle indoors, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Subway rider shot in the head by police files claim accusing officers of recklessly opening fire
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A lawyer for a New York City subway rider who was inadvertently shot in the head by police at a station has filed a legal claim against the city, accusing the officers of showing “carelessness and reckless” disregard for the lives of others when they opened fire on a man holding
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles D.A. to ‘reevaluate’ Menendez brothers’ convictions
item.description: Los Angeles County District Attorney George Gascón provided an update Thursday on the convictions of Erik and Lyle Menendez, who were convicted of killing their parents in August 1989.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Twin babies who died alongside their mother in Georgia are youngest-known Hurricane Helene victims
item.description: Obie Williams could hear babies crying and branches battering the windows when he answered his daughter’s daily phone call last week as Hurricane Helene tore through her rural Georgia town.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas casino implosion show will include 150 'Pyrodrones' 400 feet above the Tropicana
item.description: Las Vegas tourists should not run in fear as 150 “Pyrdrones” shoot sparks 400 feet above the Tropicana demolition site next week—because it’s all part of the show.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, Fox 5

item.title: California man learns sentence for killing UCLA graduate student Brianna Kupfer in brutal stabbing attack
item.description: Shawn Laval Smith, 34, was sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole on Wednesday for the 2022 killing of 24-year-old Brianna Kupfer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Harris campaigns in Wisconsin and Trump in Michigan in battle for 'blue wall' states
item.description: Democratic presidential candidate Kamala Harris is opening a two-day stay in Wisconsin and Michigan, and Republican candidate Donald Trump will be in Michigan on Thursday as the two candidates grapple for wins in the “blue wall” battleground states, which also include Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Melania Trump: ‘We are dealing with much more danger’
item.description: Former first lady Melania Trump weighed in on the state of former President Donald Trump's bid for the White House in an interview Wednesday, while also reflecting on the recent assassination attempts on her husband's life.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bought a Clif Bar? You could be paid as part of a class action settlement
item.description: A lawsuit against Clif Bar & Company alleged that Clif Bars and Clif Kid ZBars were unhealthy due to added sugars, despite being advertised with slogans like "Nutrition for Sustained Energy" and "No High Fructose Corn Syrup" on the product's packaging.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Costco now selling platinum bars and coins on website
item.description: Following the smash success of adding gold and silver bars and coins to its list of products, Costco has added a new precious metal to its stores: platinum.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: The search for the missing hits snags at every corner as Helene's death toll tops 200
item.description: Searchers are combing the remote mountains of North Carolina to locate the missing and those needing supplies nearly a week after Hurricane Helene barreled through the Southeast.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Amy, Erik Verduzco And Brittany Peterson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump calls new January 6th accusations 'pure election interference'
item.description: The former president spoke exclusively to NewsNation about the unsealing of documents related to his election interference case.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Helene's flooding swept away 11 workers at a Tennessee factory. Now the state is investigating
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Tennessee state authorities said Wednesday they were investigating the company that owns a plastics factory where 11 workers were swept away by cataclysmic flooding unleashed by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE and CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US school-entry vaccination rates fall as exemptions keep rising
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. kindergarten vaccination rates dipped last year and the proportion of children with exemptions rose to an all-time high, according to federal data posted Wednesday. The share of kids exempted from vaccine requirements rose to 3.3%, up from 3% the year before.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police delivered a 'beatdown' that killed Tyre Nichols, prosecutor says in trial closing
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Memphis police officers who beat Tyre Nichols to death wanted to punish him after he ran from a 2023 traffic stop and thought they could get away with it, a prosecutor said Wednesday during closing arguments in the federal trial of three of the officers.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Seattle judge releases accused killer 27 hours after 65-year-old military veteran stabbed to death
item.description: A judge in Washington state released a 19-year-old man accused of stabbing a 65-year-old handyman and veteran to death at a home in Renton.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man who was mad about Chinese spy balloon is convicted of threatening former Speaker McCarthy
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — A Montana man was convicted Wednesday of threatening to assault former House Speaker Kevin McCarthy after becoming upset that the government had not shot down a Chinese spy balloon that floated over his home city.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Deadly Maui fire erupted from earlier blaze believed to have been extinguished, investigation finds
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — The wildfire that killed at least 102 people on Maui last year erupted from an earlier brushfire, sparked by downed power lines, that firefighters believed they had extinguished, officials confirmed Wednesday as they presented their findings on the cause. The Aug.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Shoppers are panic-buying toilet paper amid port strike, but experts say there’s no need
item.description: “Hope you have some toilet paper stock piled. It’s started. Sam’s and Costco are depleted,” one user said on the social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KRON4

item.title: Houston teens carjack driver to smuggle migrants
item.description: Two men have pleaded guilty to a carjacking in South Texas where they said they stole a vehicle in order to smuggle migrants into the country.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: South Texas border mourns native son Kris Kristofferson
item.description: The recent death of actor and singer-songwriter Kris Kristofferson is being felt on the South Texas border where he was born and often returned to visit.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Consumers are panic-buying amid the port strike. Experts say that's not necessary
item.description: Some consumers nationwide are panic-buying goods like toilet paper amid the East Coast port workers’ strike. However, experts say that isn’t necessary. On Tuesday, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Fat Bear Week postponed due to bear vs. bear violence
item.description: Fat Bear Week is off to a violent start this year. The bracket and all the chunky contestants were scheduled to be revealed on Monday morning, but the reveal was pushed back by a day after one bear, No. 469, attacked and killed another bear, No. 402.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KTLA 5

item.title: Parents spot kids in 7-Eleven robbery footage, turn them in to police
item.description: In each instance, the crew rolled up to the stores on bicycles during the evening hours and stole items, damaged property and left without paying, police said.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: Port strike may hike prices, cause shortages of certain goods: What to know
item.description: U.S. ports from Maine to Texas shut down Tuesday when the union representing about 45,000 dockworkers went on strike for the first time since 1977.
item.dc:creator: Mae Anderson, KRON4

item.title: Tile's Back With Trackers That One-Up AirTags
item.description: Remember Tile? They were one of the first Bluetooth tracking tags, and now they have four new products. The company—fresh off its Life360 acquisition—hopes to stand out in a world of AirTags and Android Trackers with unique features.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: California firefighters in Hurricane Helene relief team recovering after crash in Texas
item.description: In a statement on Facebook, the San Diego Fire-Rescue Department thanked the community for their support during this difficult time.
item.dc:creator: Christa Swanson, Fox 5

item.title: Poll: Who won the Walz-Vance VP Debate?
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Political scientists will tell you that vice presidential nominees rarely swing the election in favor of the candidate at the top of the ticket. But poll after poll shows this election cycle coming down to a percentage point across a handful of key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5

item.title: Memorable moments from the vice presidential debate
item.description: Vice presidential nominees Sen. JD Vance (R-Ohio) and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) clashed Tuesday in what is likely to be the last debate of this presidential election season.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, KRON4

item.title: Montana man gets 6 months in prison for cloning giant sheep and breeding it
item.description: GREAT FALLS, Mont. (AP) — An 81-year-old Montana man was sentenced Monday to six months in federal prison for illegally using tissue and testicles from large sheep hunted in Central Asia and the U.S. to create hybrid sheep for captive trophy hunting in Texas and Minnesota. U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMY BETH HANSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A chemical cloud moving around Atlanta's suburbs prompts a new shelter-in-place alert
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Residents east of Atlanta were again warned Tuesday to take shelter where they are if shifting winds push the still-billowing chemical cloud from a chlorine factory fire over their neighborhood.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Watchdog blasts DEA for not reporting waterboarding, torture by Latin American partners
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — A federal government watchdog is blasting the U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration for failing to timely report human rights violations committed by Latin American law enforcement partners who admitted to waterboarding, suffocating and torturing crime suspects.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NHC tracking disturbance near where Helene formed | Tracking the Tropics
item.description: How strong it will be and where is it going to go? This afternoon on Tracking the Tropics, we'll break down what we know about the disturbance and where it could be headed.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: JD Vance, Tim Walz face off in 2024 VP debate: Key takeaways
item.description: Republican J.D. Vance and Democrat Tim Walz met Tuesday in the lone vice presidential debate of the 2024 election, bringing together undercards who have spent two months going after each other and the opposing nominees who top the major-party tickets.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FBI to pay $22M to settle claims of sexual discrimination at training academy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The FBI agreed Monday to pay more than $22 million to settle a class-action lawsuit alleging female recruits were singled out for dismissal in training and routinely harassed by instructors with sexually charged comments about their breast size, false allegations of infidelity and
item.dc:creator: JIM MUSTIAN, Associated Press, Fox 5
